HW6

2026-04-01

Initial House Prices Graph per State

The R code below generates a facet_wrap plot that separates the house_prices vs. date graphs for all states within the data. When enlarged, it can be useful for making comparisons between states.

Comparing House Prices and Unemployment Percentage per State

This code below combines the house_prices and unemploy_perc columns using gather, and then uses ggplot to graph it using the same method as the previous graph. This is not a good visualization as the values are not standardized: you can’t compare nominal house prices to a percentage value – as shown on the graph, it won’t create a good visualization without having both be percentages to make accurate comparisons.
